_description: Model derivations for France vs Paraguay 2026-07-04 R16

rule_checks.RULE7: FIRES HARD -- Paraguay win=4.67%, comparable to ARG-CPV's Cape Verde (3.51%), the most extreme underdog territory this campaign
rule_checks.RULE15_refinement_from_ARGCPV_postmortem: Paraguay's most recent game (R32 vs Germany) is a genuine COMPETITIVE-performance precedent (led 1-0, drew 1-1 in regulation, forced ET) -- NOT a blowout-victim precedent. Per the ARG-CPV lesson, do not default to blanket blowout-suppression logic for Paraguay's team-level metrics; this is a team that has repeatedly shown it competes hard in exactly this kind of lopsided knockout matchup.
rule_checks.CAN_MOR_regime_coverage_check_applied: Before blending toward any empirical 'floor' or 'ceiling', checked whether the team's own sample actually covers the plausible range of game states for today. France's SOT/corners empirical signal DOES have decent regime coverage (their best output came against their toughest opponent yet, Sweden in R32) -- unlike Canada's SOT floor, which never saw a blowout-loss game state. This supports a moderate (not full) blend toward empirical for Q6/Q10, well short of the aggressive 80% weighting that blew up on CAN-MOR.
rule_checks.ENCISO_SPECIFIC_LESSON: GER-PAR Q8 (Enciso 1+ SOT) was suppressed to 0.32 based on his 0-SOT history and LOST -26.3 RBP when he broke through in a match where Paraguay's whole attacking ceiling shattered. Applying that same caution here: NOT over-suppressing Enciso this time despite his continued 0,0,0-then-1 SOT pattern -- trusting the market's more balanced 42.48% instead of re-running the same suppression play that already backfired once on this exact player.

espn_insight.france_sot_trend: 8, 5, 9, 12 -- rising through the tournament, peak vs their best opponent (Sweden R32)
espn_insight.dembele_bimodal_pattern: 2+ SOT only vs weaker/mid opposition (Iraq, Norway); blanked vs tougher opposition (Senegal, Sweden) -- Paraguay's defense, while resilient on scoreline, allows plenty of shots on target (conceded 6 SOT to Germany even in a game they drew), so this doesn't clearly suppress Dembele today
espn_insight.paraguay_lead_precedent: Led at some point in 2 of 4 games (50%), most recently vs Germany (12 minutes) -- a real, recent, relevant data point for Q12
espn_insight.almiron_total_anonymity: 0 goals, 0 assists, 0 SOT in every game featured, missing from the Australia squad entirely -- a personal-zero signal independent of game state, distinct from the team-context Enciso case
